channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ghana's ex-President John Mahama is set to return after rival concedes election
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — Ghana’s former President John Dramani Mahama is set to return to office in the West African nation’s presidential election after the ruling party candidate, Vice President Mahamudu Bawumia, on Sunday conceded defeat and voters vented anger at the government’s handling of the econ
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: These states have the highest — and lowest — median salary: new data
item.description: New data released by a payroll site found median annual salaries from $40,000 to over six figures across the country.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: The spiritual heart of Paris awakens: Notre Dame hosts first Mass since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New photos released of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: The manhunt continues for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer as police released new photos of the suspect.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, KRON4

item.title: Jubilation, gunfire as Syrians celebrate the end of the Assad family's half-century rule
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Marrakech festival spotlights tensions animating Morocco's movie industry
item.description: MARRAKECH, Morocco (AP) — After the movie “Cabo Negro” screened at the Marrakech International Film Festival this week, organizers anticipating backlash whisked its crew away and canceled screenwriter-director Abdellah Taia’s scheduled post-film Q&A.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden adds to the nation's list of national monuments during his term. There's an appetite for more
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. President Theodore Roosevelt did in 1906 what Congress was unwilling to do through legislation: He used his new authority under the Antiquities Act to designate Devils Tower in Wyoming as the first national monument.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Giant statue of the Buddha in New Jersey becomes interfaith hub and spiritual home for many
item.description: One of the largest statues of the Buddha in the United States springs up unexpectedly in the middle of a backyard just off a busy New Jersey state highway.
item.dc:creator: Luis Andres Henao, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Search for UnitedHealthcare CEO's killer yields evidence, but few answers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They have seen him smiling on a hostel security camera, but don’t know his name. They found the backpack he discarded while fleeing, but don’t know where he's gone.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Five California Lottery players become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games, lottery officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Doorbell camera captures Illinois garbage truck explosion
item.description: Authorities described the explosion as "significant" and said truck parts were launched into the air, landing several blocks away.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What happens when 'The Simpsons' join 'Monday Night Football'? Find out during Bengals-Cowboys
item.description: On Monday night, the entire Simpsons universe gets to experience it in a way not many could have imagined. The prime-time matchup between the Cincinnati Bengals and Dallas Cowboys will also take place at Springfield’s Atoms Stadium as part of “The Simpsons Funday Football” alternate broadcast.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Settlement offers nearly $9M to Louisiana nursing home residents kept in warehouse during hurricane
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Some of the elderly residents of seven Louisiana nursing homes who were sent in 2021 to ride out Hurricane Ida in a crowded, ill-equipped warehouse are being offered shares of a nearly $9 million settlement after they sued.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President of Tyler Perry Studios killed in Florida plane crash
item.description: Reports say that Steve Mensch was piloting the plane that crashed on a road in Homosassa, Florida. He was the lone passenger.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Disneyland announces closure date for Red Car Trolley attraction
item.description: Disneyland has announced a closing date for the Red Car Trolley attraction at Disney California Adventure as construction for an expansion of Avengers Campus nears.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: 5 recipes from the pros to give after-dinner cocktails some holiday flair
item.description: From a festive Old Fashioned to a boozy hot chocolate, these after-dinner drinks might inspire your own menu of holiday cocktails, to be sipped slowly by the fire or washed down with your Christmas pudding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff’s Office between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How 'Mufasa' rose with Aaron Pierre and Blue Ivy's voices along with new Lin-Manuel Miranda music
item.description: When Aaron Pierre was cast as Mufasa, the weight of following in the late James Earl Jones’ legendary footsteps was enough to rattle any actor.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr., Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Photos show face of 'person of interest' wanted in shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The NYPD released the photos amid a city-wide manhunt for the gunman who shot UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Child rape and violent incidents reported at Massachusetts migrant shelters, former facility director says
item.description: A former director at a Massachusetts migrant shelter is speaking out against what he says were incidents of widespread violence, child sexual assault and rape at shelters in the state, including an incident where a father impregnated his 14-year-old daughter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio bill would require high-demand liquor bottles to be opened when sold
item.description: The bill would require liquor stores to open the bottle and reseal it upon a sale to curb the secondary resell market of high-end spirits.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: MAP: Flu season is here, especially in these states
item.description: Flu activity spiked in states across the U.S. during the last week of November, according to a new report from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dallas Police employee fired after allegedly lying about college degree
item.description: An employee at the Dallas Police Department was fired over allegations of lying about her college degree to receive a promotion, which is a class B misdemeanor in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lakefront family massacre suspect's defense demands apology for 'false' Clorox accusation
item.description: The defense team for a teen boy, charged with killing all but one member of his family in their Washington state lakefront home in October, denied accusations of crime scene tampering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: A pair of ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen nearly two decades ago have fetched $28 million at auction.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other, according to testimony Friday by an officer who helped lead the investigation.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Congo government 'on alert' over mystery flu-like disease that killed dozens
item.description: “First diagnostics are leading us to think it is a respiratory disease,” an official said. “But we need to wait for the laboratory results.
item.dc:creator: Jean-Yves Kamale, Fox 5

item.title: The Associated Press’ top photos of 2024 from the US
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The right frame can freeze a moment in time, creating meaning for the masses from a fist pump over a bloodied ear, a bridge crumbled by a ship, towns shredded by nature, and a victory sealed with an on-field kiss. In 2024, photographers across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 kindergarteners wounded and gunman dead after shooting at California religious school
item.description: PALERMO, Calif. (AP) — Two children were in “extremely critical condition” Wednesday after being shot at a tiny religious K-8 school in Northern California and the gunman died at the scene, apparently from a self-inflicted gunshot, police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jury revisits key videos in NYC subway chokehold death trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors asked Wednesday to review police and bystander video at the heart of the chokehold manslaughter case against Daniel Penny as his lawyers complained that the Marine veteran was being harassed outside the New York City courthouse.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Union rights take center stage in already high-stakes Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's state Supreme Court election next spring already had high stakes, with majority control on the line.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court Latest: Majority of justices appear likely to uphold state ban on transgender care
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments on Wednesday in just its second major transgender rights case, a challenge to a Tennessee law that bans gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US border arrests drop 17% in November, dousing predictions of post-election surge
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — U.S. authorities made about 46,700 arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in November, down about 17% from October to a new low for Joe Biden's presidency, an official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4
